evidence.assertion: All capabilities required by released patterns have an explicit adapter mapping and executable or typed fallback contract.
Normal, replay, skip, and resume paths commit one durable cue effect and restore control.
Every released effect instance resolves through an effectId-specific typed transaction with complete parameter, precondition, postcondition, and rollback attestation.
Every changed gate preserves required goal and recovery reachability; a trapping gate is rejected without mutation.
Irreversible effects reject before their gates, commit once after the gate, and survive save reload without duplication.
Tile, collision, prop, interaction, portal, lighting, and route domains commit atomically and interrupted commits roll back exactly.
NPC, dialogue, schedule, inventory, object placement, collision, and composition stay transactionally coherent.
Portal target, spawn, facing, checkpoint, carried state, collision safety, and return route close before commit.
Checkpoint, save reload, and migration preserve every authoritative domain and transaction ledger exactly.

evidence.fallback: Use an on-map focus pulse without moving the camera.
Use an authored single target at the same story-derived location.
Select one concise before line and one after line from the same fact gate.
Use persistent facts only for non-presented and non-consumable roles.
Use a bounded authored zone delta with the same collision, interaction, portal, prop, and lighting closure.
Use one before and one after authored NPC state that read the same authoritative fact.
Use two or more authored socket combinations that preserve the same role algebra.
Offer authored sockets derived from the same object-role rule.
Use one sprite action and text cue with the same durable transaction.
Use a same-map threshold with the same safe-cell and return-edge contract.
Use one authored threshold edge with the same fact condition and recovery node.
Reject an incompatible save with a recoverable message; never silently discard authoritative state.
Use authored before and after schedule windows keyed by the same world fact.
Use authored bounded zones when free spatial queries exceed the product budget.
Omit metric-only events while preserving gameplay state; telemetry may never be a progression dependency.
